title: SOULCALIBUR VI

gpu: ATI Technologies Inc. AMD Radeon R9 200 Series
notes:
FMV does not work. 2D Cut-scenes and UI elements work fine. Yellow filter over everything that is rendered in game. Performance is fine minus some stutter problems. tried Esync disable and Wind d3d11. Wine d3d fixes the graphics issues however player models are dark and sometimes ghost in character select. Performance on Wine d3d is Horrible do not recommend at all.

Did not test story or libre of souls. Arcade seems to work fine have not gone further than stage 2.
title: SOULCALIBUR VI

notes: FMV cutscenes don't play at all (I've noticed this seems to be a trend with Unreal Engine). On first launch, the game runs fine otherwise. After following a post to symlink the libgnutls files, network mode works. Tested Arcade, Libra of Souls, and Soul Chronicle and everything works fine. After closing and running the game again, however, I keep getting an error that the GameThread timed out. It seems that this is a known issue for some Windows users, but I'm not sure if it's something with DXVK or bad luck. If I reinstall the game, it works fine on first launch again, but the same thing happens when trying to run it again afterward. Sometimes I can disable ESync, but that rarely works. I've had better luck getting it to run a second (and third) time if I use Wine D3d11, but as mentioned below there's a harder performance hit and the models look dark (as if the lighting is ignoring them). Here's the post that mentions how to fix networking (it's for fixing UPlay, but fixes SC6 too): https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/1757#issuecomment-431571624
title: SOULCALIBUR VI

notes:
The video cutscenes still doesn't work, and I haven't find a solution to this yet. Maybe someone will come with a solution to this.

However, the online issues are now fixed on Proton 3.16-4 as it was a problem on Proton itself sending corrupted data to the game servers. So you can now play the game online with no problems, I tested playing against other people, and it was great, there is only one thing tho, the game couldn't seem to recognize the region I was in, it was showing the mapa mundi at the side of my name. So I don't know if it was just a bug or if it can't actually recognize my region.

Performance wise the game runs pretty good, there is a little performance hit tho, but nothing big, lowering my scale setting from 125 to 120 gave me almost rock solid 60fps.

All game modes are working, with the only exception being Chronicles where you will need to launch it one single time on Windows to get past de first cutscene, after that it should work.
title: SOULCALIBUR VI

notes: Proton 3.16-4 seems to have fixed the connection error, and I was even able to join a lobby and play a few online matches. Performance is good with no obvious graphical errors and Xinput/Steam controller mappings are fully supported. The only thing keeping me from rating this Platinum is the lack of video cut scene playback, most obviously in the title screen.
title: SOULCALIBUR VI

notes: I tried the network features again after proton 3.16-4, and found that the rankings and replays work fine, but I'm unable to find a match online in both casual and ranked.
title: SOULCALIBUR VI

notes: Cut scenes don't work. Network works with proton 3.16-4, but the game doesn't recognize my region properly, so it puts me into some "world" region or something, which makes waiting for casual and ranked games very long as the default setting is to find other players in the same region. I found 1 ranked match during 30 mins of testing. Once the match started everything ran smoothly.
title: SOULCALIBUR VI

notes: Performance is excellent, and all modes (versus, training, Libra of Souls, Soul Chronicle) are playable without issue. Online multiplayer runs great, but finding a match in ranked seems to be taking a lot longer in Proton for some reason. Cutscenes and the title screen does not work.
